title: Injection sink review
description: Trace untrusted input into each interpreter and prove a sink-specific safe API is used.
body: Use for SQL, shell, template, path, URL redirect, XML, archive extraction, eval, or deserialization changes. Do not use to approve generic sanitizers without a sink contract. Steps: label the trust source, transformations, canonicalization, sink, and output context; replace string assembly with parameterized or structured APIs; add malicious input tests that exercise separators, encodings, and nested payloads. Validation signals include the dangerous payload treated as data or rejected. Risk boundary: if the sink executes code or reaches internal network resources, require human review. Use when Security Risk Controls work touches trust boundaries, authorization decisions, dangerous sinks, secrets, and dependency execution. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include negative security tests, server-side checks, sink payload tests, and secret scans. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves client-only controls, injection, credential leakage, permissive fallback, and unsafe dependency changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
